content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacrami:nlo, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
October 23, 2012 
Mr. Dominic T. Holzhaus 
Principal Deputy City Attorney 
333 West Ocean Blvd., 11th Floor 
Long Beach, CA 90802-4664 
Re: 
Your Request for lnfonnal Assistance 
Our file No. 1-12-144 
Dear Mr. Holzhaus: 
This letter responds to your request for infonnal assistance on behalf of Commissioner 
Rich Dines regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Refonn Act (the "Act .. ).1 
Because your question is general in nature, we are treating your request as one for informal 
. 
.., 
assistance. -
As Principal Deputy City Attorney for the city of Long Beach, you represent Harbor 
Commissioner Rich Dines in a follow-up question to our 2011 letter to you, Advice Letter 1-11-
153 ("2011 Letter"). The facts and analysis are nearly unchanged since our last letter; this letter 
therefore responds only to whether the changed facts affect the analysis. 
QUESTION 
Is the 2011 Letter to Commissioner Dines still accurate where the commissioner left his 
job as a longshoreman and now works as a line-handler for the National Lines Bureau, Inc. 
("NLB .. ) (although he continues to be paid through the Pacific Maritime Association (PMA))? 
CONCLUSION 
The 2011 Letter's analysis still applies. The NLB. as a PMA member, is the 
Commissioner's source of income. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are 10 Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indica1ed. 
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
wriuen advice. (Section 83114: Regulacion l8329(c)(3).) 


DISCUSSION 
File No. 1-12-144 
Page No. 2 
When last you wrote, Commissioner Dines worked as a longshoreman for tenants of the 
Port of Long Beach, paid by the tenants but through the Pacific Maritime Association ( .. PMA"). 
At this time, and for the last five months, he has transitioned his job to that of a line-handler. 
Line handlers tie and untie ships calling at the port. As a line handler, Mr. Dines works for the 
National Lines Bureau, Inc. ( .. NLB") and continues to be paid through the PMA. The work is 
governed by a separate agreement between PMA and the International Longshore and 
Warehouse Union ("IL WU"). 
NLB operates to provide line handling to ships calling at the Port of Long Beach. When 
a ship seeks to come into port, it contacts its local agent who arranges the services needed by that 
ship while in port. Among those calls is one to NLB for line handlers. Once the job is complete, 
NLB reports the hours worked to PMA and PMA issues a check to the line handler. At this time, 
Mr. Dines is solely a line handler for NLB and is not sent out on other assignments when PMA 
directs, as in his previous position. 
Our 2011 Letter determined that Mr. Dines' source of income was not PMA, but those 
member companies that contract with PMA to provide services at the Port of Long Beach. 
Because NLB is one of those member companies that contracts with PMA, our analysis in the 
2011 Letter still applies here. That is: Mr. Dines' source of income is now NLB. We agree with 
your assertion that Mr. Dines' current situation is more in line with the facts in the Sauer Advice 
Letter, A-95-373. For a discussion of the Sauer Letter, see our 2011 Letter. 
The rest of the 2011 Letter's analysis applies as to NLB as a member company and 
Commissioner Dines' source of income. Additionally, Commissioner Dines also has an 
economic interest in any source of income, as discussed in the 201 l Letter, from which he 
received at least $500 in the last 12 months. You have not offered information on any particular 
governmental decision before Commissioner Dines, so we have limited our response to 
addressing those changed facts since the 2011 Letter. Please refer to that letter for further 
analysis. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
HMR:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
JJ/JNV(L,f\__ 
By: 
Heather M. Rowan 
Counsel, Legal Division
